pages.text:
23/730

F.A. 43,

DEPARTEMENT VAN BUITELANDSE SAKE

DEPARTMENT OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS

CAPE TOWN.

8ix,

I have the honour to refer to the recent nego-

tiations which took place in Pretoria betreen representatives of the Goverumenta of France and of the Republio of south

Africa, in the course of which it was agrood that Artiele 9 o1 the Air Agreement which was entered into betreen our two Governments on the 17th September, 1954, be further amended by:

(a) the substitution in paragraph (1) Lor the words "eighty-eight" of the nords "ona- bundred and five"; and (b)

the subatitution in sub-paragraph (a) oI paragraph (2) for the words "eighty-eight" of the words "one-hundred and five".

I have the honour to infor» you that the Government

of the Republic of South Africa agrees to the arendments apacified abore, and I should be pleased to learn whether the Government of france likewise agrees to these amendments.

If so, I auggest that thia letter and your confirnatory roply thereto, aball be regarded an constituting an agreement betreon our tro Gorernments.

Ploane accept, Sir, the renewed assurance of my

high consideration.

SECRETARY FOR FOREIGN APPAIRS.

hr. c. Beguin-B111ac00g,

Chargé d'Affaires of France,

CAPE TOWN.

.
